name: scikit-survival (ML-Based Survival Analysis)

performance.notes: Cython/sklearn backend; tree methods fast; gradient boosting can be slow

agent_readiness.notes: Machine learning survival analysis with scikit-learn API. from sksurv.linear_model import CoxPHSurvivalAnalysis. from sksurv.ensemble import RandomSurvivalForest, GradientBoostingSurvivalAnalysis. from sksurv.util import Surv. y = Surv.from_dataframe('event', 'time', df). est = RandomSurvivalForest(n_estimators=100).fit(X, y). c_index = est.score(X_test, y_test). est.predict_cumulative_hazard_function(X_test). est.predict_survival_function(X_test). sklearn Pipeline compatible. LASSO Cox: CoxnetSurvivalAnalysis.

gotchas: Target format (structured array via Surv.from_dataframe) is non-standard
Not all sklearn features work (e.g., GridSearchCV needs custom scorer)
Gradient boosting can be very slow for large datasets
best_when: Machine learning-based survival analysis (Random Survival Forest, GBSA)
When Cox PH linearity assumption doesn't hold
scikit-learn pipeline integration for survival models
avoid_when: When lifelines' friendlier API is sufficient
summary: scikit-survival provides ML-based survival analysis with scikit-learn API. Surv.from_dataframe('event', 'time', df). RandomSurvivalForest().fit(X, y). GradientBoostingSurvivalAnalysis. CoxnetSurvivalAnalysis (LASSO). predict_survival_function(). sklearn Pipeline compatible. GPL-3.0 by Sebastian Pölsterl. Best for non-linear survival models where Cox PH fails. GPL copyleft for commercial use. Use lifelines for simple KM/Cox workflows.
